mission.title: keycloak: Support configurable custom Identity Providers
mission.description: Support configurable custom Identity Providers. Requested by 23+ users.

steps.title: Check current Keycloak setup
steps.description:
Verify your Keycloak version and installed providers:
```bash
# If running in a container:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config

# List installed providers:
docker exec keycloak ls /opt/keycloak/providers/
```
Custom Identity Provider support requires Keycloak 22+ with the ConfiguredProvider interface improvements.

steps.title: Understand the custom IDP requirement
steps.description:
Keycloak's Identity Provider system originally did not use the `ConfiguredProvider` interface, which meant custom IDPs could not expose configurable properties through the Admin Console UI. Developers had to extend the Admin Console itself to add configuration fields for custom IDPs.

The `ConfiguredProvider` interface allows providers to declare their configuration properties (name, type, help text, default values), which Keycloak then renders automatically in the Admin Console. This feature brings that capability to Identity Providers, both Social and User-defined types.

steps.title: Implement a configurable custom Identity Provider
Verify the custom IDP appears in the Admin Console
steps.description:
After deploying your custom IDP provider:
1. Open the Keycloak Admin Console
2. Go to **Identity Providers**
3. Click **Add provider** and select your custom IDP from the list
4. Verify that the custom configuration fields you defined appear in the configuration form
5. Fill in the configuration and save
6. Test a login flow using your custom IDP to confirm it works end-to-end

resolution.summary: Keycloak added support for the ConfiguredProvider interface on Identity Providers, allowing custom IDPs to declare configurable properties that are automatically rendered in the Admin Console. This eliminates the need to extend the Admin Console UI for custom IDP configuration. Both Social (SocialIdentityProviderFactory) and User-defined (IdentityProviderFactory) providers can use this interface.

prerequisites.description: A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes with the Keycloak Operator) and Java development tools for building custom providers.
